Details: Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by direct filtration can be very effective (up to 3-log reduction) where the effectiveness is dependent on turbidity removal. Removal of Cryptosporidium through direct filtration has been shown to be comparable to conventional treatment and has been proven to be the most effective due to consistent and proper chemical coagulation and flocculation. Direct filtration is cited in LT1 as an effective means, based on effective turbidity control, of Cryptosporidium removal.
